name: Cannon AFB
profile: Military-friendly Cannon AFB housing snapshot anchored to Clovis, Portales, and eastern New Mexico, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Affordable rural military market with slower velocity and strong payment accessibility

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on March 2026 New Mexico VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Cannon is a low-entry-price market, so buyers should avoid overbuying simply because the purchase price looks manageable.
Insurance, utilities, older-home maintenance, wind exposure, and rural-market resale should be included in all-in cost comparisons.
Lower taxes and lower home values can make ownership accessible for disciplined VA buyers.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan planning estimate for Clovis / Cannon-area home values.

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military installation with controlled access.
crime_status.metro_clovis_portales: Clovis and Portales require neighborhood-level screening; families should compare safety, school zones, property condition, and commute rather than relying only on affordability.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Clovis Municipal Schools and Portales-area schools vary by address; families should verify school zones and program fit before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by military turnover, rural market depth, and the limited number of larger single-family rentals.

rental_metrics.notes: Planning estimate based on Clovis-area rental observations, with 3-bedroom single-family rents often driving the military-family decision.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Cannon has a tight-knit special operations and rural military community, with low commute stress, affordable housing, and fewer big-city amenities.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Cannon is one of the most affordable Air Force markets by purchase price, but buyers should still account for limited resale depth, rural amenities, property condition, and assignment length.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers comparing Clovis, Portales, and select newer or better-maintained neighborhoods.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Cannon offers unusually accessible home prices for military buyers, but the best decision depends on assignment length, resale risk, neighborhood quality, and whether the user is comfortable with a rural lifestyle.

summary_points: Cannon AFB is one of the more affordable Air Force housing markets by purchase price.
Clovis and Portales offer low-cost housing options, but property condition and neighborhood quality vary materially.
The rural lifestyle is a major factor; users should evaluate amenities, spouse employment, schools, healthcare access, and assignment length.
BAH can often support buying for disciplined households, but resale depth and exit strategy should be reviewed before purchase.
Cannon works best for buyers who prioritize payment control, commute simplicity, and practical housing over big-city lifestyle.
opportunities: Very low purchase prices compared with most U.S. military markets.
Low commute pressure can improve daily quality of life.
VA buyers may find ownership accessible with controlled debt and realistic target price.
Military turnover supports rental demand for clean, practical housing.
Clovis and Portales can offer larger homes at lower monthly cost than high-cost Air Force metros.
risks: Rural-market resale depth can be thinner than larger metros.
Lifestyle fit may be challenging for families expecting big-city amenities.
Older housing stock can create maintenance exposure.
Neighborhood quality and school fit vary enough to require address-level review.
Short assignments can make transaction costs and resale risk more important.
buyer_notes: Cannon is attractive for buyers who want a low payment and are comfortable with eastern New Mexico’s rural lifestyle.
Do not buy simply because the home price looks low; test resale, condition, neighborhood, and assignment length.
Compare Clovis and Portales by commute, schools, services, and family fit.
A clean, well-located home with manageable maintenance may be more valuable than the cheapest available property.
This market rewards buyers who preserve residual income and avoid assuming fast appreciation.
seller_notes: Sellers should price realistically because buyers have affordability options and market velocity can be slower.
Move-in-ready condition, roof/HVAC clarity, and clean presentation matter strongly.
Military-friendly positioning should emphasize commute simplicity, payment practicality, and low-maintenance living.
Homes with good condition and strong neighborhood fit may stand out more than homes relying only on low price.
investor_angles: Military renter demand can support stable occupancy for clean, well-located homes.
Low acquisition cost can improve cash-flow potential.
Investors should stress-test vacancy, maintenance, tenant turnover, and rural-market resale.
Best suited for yield-focused investors who understand base-driven demand and conservative underwriting.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly cost as the primary decision filter.
Compare Clovis, Portales, and Cannon-adjacent options by commute, school zones, safety, condition, and resale.
Verify roof, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, insulation, and wind/dust-related maintenance exposure before buying.
Avoid choosing solely by lowest price.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, assignment length, and exit risk.
seller_guidance: Price against current buyer payment sensitivity and slower rural-market velocity.
Highlight commute simplicity, updated systems, VA-friendly condition, and low-maintenance features.
Provide clear information on roof, HVAC, utilities, and property condition.
Military buyers need practical monthly value and confidence in the exit plan.
landlord_notes: Military demand can support occupancy stability when the property is clean, safe, and priced correctly.
Cash flow may be stronger than appreciation potential.
Investors should model maintenance and turnover conservatively.
Homes with short commutes, functional layouts, and clean condition are better positioned for military tenants.

market.market_type_summary: Affordable rural military market with slower resale velocity

base_profile.installation_type: Special Operations / Air Force Special Operations Command Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 27th Special Operations Wing / Air Force Special Operations Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Cannon AFB is an Air Force Special Operations Command installation supporting special operations aviation, readiness, training, global mobility, and mission support in eastern New Mexico.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Rural, affordable, mission-focused, tight-knit, commute-light, amenity-limited, and strongly tied to Clovis and Portales housing decisions.
base_profile.base_bluf: Cannon is not just a low-cost housing decision. It is a rural-lifestyle, assignment-length, resale-risk, BAH-fit, and family-support decision. The low purchase prices can be powerful, but only when the user chooses the right property and understands the exit strategy.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, spouse/family lifestyle fit, commute simplicity, property condition, and resale risk before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize affordability, short commute, rental flexibility, roommate options, and access to daily services in Clovis or Portales.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Cannon housing conversations are about low payment, realistic resale, rural lifestyle, neighborhood confidence, property condition, and assignment length.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Cannon is influenced by military turnover, limited quality inventory, affordable acquisition costs, and the need for clean, functional housing near Clovis and Portales.

base_map_image.alt: PCSUnited public-facing Cannon AFB placeholder orientation image for base housing and relocation planning.
base_map_image.title: Cannon AFB Base & Housing Overview
base_map_image.caption: Placeholder PCSUnited orientation image until a dedicated Cannon gate and neighborhood map is available.
base_map_image.safety_note: This is not an official DoD map. It is a placeholder PCSUnited orientation image and should not be used for access control, emergency routing, or official navigation.

visitor_control_center.name: Cannon AFB Visitor Control Center
visitor_control_center.hours: Open 7 days a week, 6:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.; verify current hours before travel
best_for: Family members without routine installation access
visitor_control_center.note: Use official Cannon AFB visitor information before arrival. Access requirements, sponsor rules, gate procedures, and hours can change.

gates.name: Main Gate
gates.hours: Commonly reported as 24/7; verify official Cannon AFB gate hours before travel
gates.commute_notes: Primary access point for many users approaching from Clovis and US 60/84.
gates.user_warning: Verify current gate status with official Cannon AFB sources before travel.

gates.name: Portales Gate
gates.hours: Commonly reported as 24/7; verify official Cannon AFB gate hours before travel
best_for: Users approaching from south or southeast corridors
gates.commute_notes: Useful for users living in Portales or approaching from southern/eastern corridors.
gates.user_warning: Do not assume availability or best routing without checking current gate procedures.

gates.name: Commercial Gate
gates.hours: Verify official Cannon AFB commercial access procedures
gates.commute_notes: Useful for commercial or delivery planning, not a default family commute gate.
gates.user_warning: Movers and commercial deliveries should verify procedures through official base access guidance.

major_services.name: Cannon Medical Clinic
major_services.address: Verify current clinic address before travel
major_services.phone: Verify through official TRICARE / Cannon medical source
major_services.hours: Verify current clinic and pharmacy hours
major_services.user_note: Treat this as a major medical anchor for PCS planning. Verify emergency care options, pharmacy procedures, and clinic availability directly with official medical sources.

major_services.name: Cannon Commissary
major_services.address: Verify current commissary address before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with DeCA
major_services.user_note: Commissary access can materially affect monthly food budget for eligible users, especially in a rural market with fewer nearby large-city shopping options.

major_services.name: Cannon AFB Main Exchange
major_services.address: 102 Levitow Ave, Bldg. 77B, Cannon AFB, NM 88103
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with AAFES
major_services.hours: Mon-Fri 0800-1800; Sat-Sun 1000-1700 per Exchange store locator; verify current hours
major_services.user_note: Important daily-life anchor for new arrivals, families, and permanent party.

major_services.name: Military Clothing Store
major_services.user_note: Useful for military-specific shopping needs after arrival.

major_services.name: Cannon AFB Military Housing Office
major_services.address: 401 James Boatwright, Chavez Housing Area, Cannon AFB, NM 88101
major_services.hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.; verify current hours
major_services.user_note: Military families should contact the housing office before committing to on-base or off-base housing.

major_services.name: Cannon Family Homes / Balfour Beatty
major_services.address: 401 James Boatwright, Chavez Housing Area, Cannon AFB, NM 88103
major_services.hours: Monday-Friday 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.; Wednesday 8:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m.; verify current hours
major_services.user_note: On-base housing can be attractive at Cannon because it reduces rural-market uncertainty and simplifies commute, but families should compare BAH capture, pet policies, waitlists, and off-base affordability.

major_services.name: Unaccompanied Housing Office
major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel
major_services.phone: Verify current phone number with official installation sources
major_services.email: Verify current email with official installation sources
major_services.user_note: Eligibility and availability should be verified directly with the housing office.

major_services.name: Bank / Credit Union / ATM Services
major_services.address: Verify current branch or ATM location before travel
major_services.user_note: Specific bank or credit union locations may change. Use current official branch locator or base directory before routing users.

major_services.name: Legal / Finance / Admin Services
major_services.address: Verify current office location before travel
major_services.user_note: Use this as a planning category in the Base tab; exact office routing should point to official base directory links.

major_services.name: Child / Youth / Family Support Services
major_services.address: Verify current CDC, youth center, and school liaison locations before travel
major_services.user_note: Critical for families. Exact program availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified with official Cannon resources.

on_base_housing.housing_type: Privatized and military housing support environment
on_base_housing.housing_office_address: 401 James Boatwright, Chavez Housing Area, Cannon AFB, NM 88101
on_base_housing.housing_office_hours: Monday-Friday, 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m.; verify current hours
on_base_housing.map_zone: housing support area / Chavez Housing Area
best_for: Families wanting to reduce rural-market uncertainty
Users who want predictable commute and base services access
watchouts: Availability and waitlists can change
On-base housing may capture BAH differently than off-base renting
Pet policies and breed restrictions should be verified
School assignment must be verified by exact address
Off-base affordability may be strong enough that families should compare both paths carefully
Rural lifestyle and spouse employment should be considered before choosing off-base distance
on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. Around Cannon, the best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, debt, commute tolerance, school priorities, family support needs, pet needs, spouse employment, and expected assignment length.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: North Clovis
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Practical Clovis option with access to services, shopping, schools, and manageable commute to Cannon.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 15-25 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and report time
likely_gate_logic: Other gates depending on duty location and current access rules
watchouts: Property condition can vary
School assignment should be verified by exact address
Resale depends heavily on condition and neighborhood quality
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Strong first-look area for users wanting practical access to Clovis services and manageable commute.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: West Clovis
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Closer-to-base Clovis area that can offer short commute potential and practical housing options.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 10-20 minutes depending on exact address and gate
likely_gate_logic: Other gates depending on duty location
watchouts: Neighborhood quality should be checked at the block level
Older homes may need maintenance reserves
Do not choose solely based on proximity
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Good for commute-first users if the exact property condition and neighborhood fit are strong.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Portales
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: College-town style option with access to Eastern New Mexico University, a different lifestyle feel, and Portales-side commute logic.
best_for: Users wanting a smaller college-town feel
Buyers who value quiet and affordability
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-35 minutes depending on exact address, gate, and route
likely_gate_logic: Main Gate depending on route and current access rules
watchouts: Longer drive than many Clovis options
Inventory and property type may be limited
Route and gate access should be verified
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Useful alternative for users who prefer Portales lifestyle or pricing and accept the commute tradeoff.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Cannon-adjacent Housing Areas
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Very short commute potential, but users should compare services, lifestyle, school access, and resale carefully.
best_for: Users wanting to minimize daily driving
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 5-15 minutes depending on exact housing location and gate
likely_gate_logic: Portales Gate depending on exact route
watchouts: Limited inventory
Lifestyle may feel isolated
Property condition and resale should be reviewed carefully
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Best for users prioritizing commute and simplicity over broader amenities.

recommended_neighborhoods.name: Select Rural Edges
recommended_neighborhoods.fit_summary: Can offer space and affordability, but requires stronger due diligence on utilities, commute, internet, roads, and resale.
recommended_neighborhoods.commute_band: Approx. 20-45+ minutes depending on exact property and road conditions
watchouts: Utilities, internet, wells, septic, roads, and maintenance must be verified
Resale may be thinner
Commute and weather exposure can matter more than expected
recommended_neighborhoods.pcsu_recommendation: Only recommended for users who intentionally want rural living and understand maintenance, utility, and resale risks.

commute_intelligence.overall_commute_posture: Very favorable compared with most military markets because traffic is light and the surrounding area is compact.
primary_commute_risks: Choosing by lowest price instead of actual lifestyle fit
Longer drives from Portales or rural edges
Wind, dust, weather, and rural-road conditions
Limited services near more isolated properties
planning_guidance: Test the commute during the user’s expected report-time window.
Confirm which gate best supports the user’s duty location.
Compare Clovis, Portales, and near-base areas by route, services, schools, safety, and resale.
Use commute plus all-in housing cost as the core PCSUnited decision screen.
commute_intelligence.pcsu_bluf: Cannon commute pressure is low, but the right choice depends on lifestyle fit, resale risk, family needs, and the quality of the specific property.

bah_market_reality.overall_position: Strongly favorable for disciplined buyers, with rural-market caution.
bah_market_reality.buying_power_summary: Low purchase prices give many Cannon families realistic buying power, especially compared with most Air Force markets.
bah_market_reality.renting_summary: Renting remains practical for shorter assignments, users uncertain about rural lifestyle fit, or households that do not want resale exposure.
bah_market_reality.pcsu_strategy_note: Cannon should be modeled as a strong affordability market, but PCSUnited should still test each user’s rank, BAH, debt, savings, credit score, timeline, family needs, and exit plan before recommending buy versus rent.

strengths: On-base housing can simplify the PCS
Clovis and Portales offer practical local services
watchouts: Rural lifestyle may not fit every family
School assignments and program fit should be verified
Spouse employment options may be more limited than larger metros
Healthcare and specialty services may require planning
Older homes can increase maintenance exposure
family_readiness.pcsu_family_bluf: Cannon can work well for families who value affordability, simplicity, and community, but it requires honest lifestyle screening and a clear housing exit strategy.

arrival_checklist: Verify current gate hours and visitor access procedures through official Cannon AFB sources.
Confirm whether on-base housing or off-base housing better fits rank, dependents, BAH, pets, school needs, spouse employment, and expected assignment length.
Compare Clovis, Portales, and Cannon-adjacent housing using commute, schools, safety, services, and all-in monthly cost.
Review roof, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, insulation, internet availability, and wind/dust-related maintenance exposure before buying.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Check whether the household is comfortable with the rural lifestyle before buying.
Use PCSUnited calculators to compare rent, buy, residual income, assignment length, and exit risk before committing.
pcs_watchouts: Do not buy only because the price is low.
Do not ignore resale risk in a smaller rural market.
Do not assume every cheap property is a good VA purchase.
Verify schools, internet, utilities, and property condition before committing.
Short assignments may favor renting or on-base housing if resale risk is high.
Lifestyle fit matters heavily at Cannon because larger-city amenities are limited.

realtor_intelligence.best_client_positioning: Lead with affordability, exit strategy, property condition, commute simplicity, and lifestyle fit. Cannon clients need grounded guidance, not hype.
buyer_consultation_angles: Rent versus buy based on assignment length
All-in payment including taxes, insurance, utilities, and maintenance
Property condition and VA appraisal readiness
Future resale appeal to military buyers
realtor_intelligence.pcsu_realtor_bluf: A strong Cannon realtor should sound like a payment strategist, property-condition filter, and rural-market exit planner — not just a sales agent.

notes: Market and housing values are PCSUnited planning estimates and should be refreshed periodically.
Gate, visitor, service, medical, and housing office details should be verified against official installation sources before publication or use in routing.
Placeholder map image is used until a dedicated Cannon base orientation map is created.
Cannon-specific public market data is thinner than larger metros, so Clovis and Curry County context should be used carefully.

source_notes: Use official Cannon AFB sources for gate hours, visitor control, newcomer guidance, and installation access.
Use official Cannon housing, Air Force Housing, and Cannon Family Homes sources for on-base housing and privatized housing details.
Use DeCA, AAFES, and TRICARE sources for commissary, exchange, and medical details.
Use Clovis-area MLS, Zillow-style indexes, Redfin-style market data, rental listing aggregators, and Census-style demographic sources for housing and market refreshes.
base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited base profiles are planning tools only. They are not official DoD, Air Force, installation, legal, financial, real estate, lending, or navigation guidance. Users should verify gate access, housing rules, school zones, medical services, visitor procedures, market prices, insurance estimates, commute conditions, and property condition through official and professional sources before making decisions.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Curry County / Clovis-area planning estimates

rent.provider: Rentometer / Apartments.com / local rental listing estimates

home_price.provider: Zillow / Redfin / Realtor.com / Clovis-area housing estimates

base_profile.provider: PCSUnited planning profile using public-facing installation, housing, and market research structure

parser_notes: Built in the PCSUnited city/base JSON pattern used by Lackland, Andrews, Barksdale, and Beale.
All major PCSUnited fields included and adapted for Cannon AFB.
Compatible with PCS Snapshot, Base Demographics Shell, Base SaaS, and TOR parsing.
Preserves duplicate value aliases for avg_home_value, average_home_value, avgHome, and city_avg_home.
Preserves duplicate median price aliases for median_list_price, median_sold_price, and median_sale_price.
Preserves by_bedroom entries for 2, 3, 4, and 5-bedroom Market Insight compatibility.

Expanded base_profile to include map image, official links, visitor control center, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market reality, family readiness, arrival checklist, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and disclaimer.
